reviews.review_msg: Her lectures are quite lengthy, but they have all the content you need to succeed in the course. Slides were basically all the content, so you didn't even have to take notes. There's no point in going to lectures, but attendance is mandatory. Exams were straightforward, but "written assignments" are legit quizzes (worth 25%)

Nicole was fine - she clearly knew her stuff and articulated the concepts well enough to make them understandable to students. However, her grading scheme is not the best. Midterm: 25%, Final: 35%, and written quizzes: 25% (the lowest one dropped). Overall, it's fine if you are good with tests, not the best professor.
what i thought would be my favorite class became my worst class. avoid at all costs. she does not know how to teach.

She does attendance marks with codes you can only attain in lecture so show up to class. Her lecture slides include maybe 60 slides per lesson and she won't go over them all... (idk). She kinda skims through the lectures because of "lack of time" and expects you to re-read them at home. Other than that she's very nice and easy to talk to.

I don't get why Nicole has such a low rating. She's honestly so sweet and kind. Yeah, her lecture slides are long, but she only makes them that long so you don't have to touch the textbook. Her exams are actually pretty easy if you just go through the slides. Overall, she's super fair and way better than her rating shows.

Lectures are pointless, she's insanely woke, and it's so tough to listen to her talk. At least 80 slides per lecture, and all she does is read off them and go on tangents about her personal life. She seems like a nice person, but the lectures are awful, unfortunately.

Nicole is a passionate prof, class is a good perspective, and tests are fair. Going to class is absolutely useless; you can study on your own, but attendance is 15% of the grade. Class is boring and content-heavy, and Nicole wears black every day (it's her fav colour)

Nicole is a passionate prof but doesn't know how to teach, unfortunately. Lectures are 75+ slides in 50 mins, and she will just read off the slides. Attending class is 15% of the grade, so everyone went to class for the mark, but did not even pay attention. Go easy on yourself and don't take a course with her unless you have to!

I don't write too many on RMP, but I am severely concerned about Nicole's rating. Nicole's lectures were very insightful and the marking was clear and fair.

I would take Nicole's course 100% again.

Do not get fooled by the rating. Almost everyone in the class was satisfied with the course.

She is a nice professor. Last term, she decided to use us as her guinea pigs in trying a new teaching technique. 60+ slides per class and expects you to read the chapters of the textbook. Her tests are unfair and not even close to the examples she posts. Final exam was "supposed" to be easy; everyone got either got an F or C+ on it. Terrible.

she doesnt know how to teach seminar classes. very inexperienced and provides little to no feedback on assignments. refused to give feedback on presentations or participation, grading is unfair and borderline arbitrary. nice as a person though
Nicole Catherine was such a lovely prof to have. She tried her best to engage with her students and is so kind and caring. If you ever get the opportunity to take a course with her, do it!
shes the nicest person but very inexperienced prof and not fit to teach a 4th year seminar. avoid at all cost

It was quite difficult to get a good grade in this class as it was graded by few things and Dr. Catherine was an extremely tough grader. She provides thorough feedback but even if you follow all of her suggestions and adjust your assignments accordingly, your grade will barely change (might even go down). Also did not any post grades on Canvas

I was honestly expecting to receive a much higher grade for a seminar class in this size. I think there were only 18 people in the class but only one person received an A- to my knowledge (which is the average grade for seminar classes). Very hard marker, very vague feedback and does not provide any of the presentation grades

she's a nice person but man, she did NOT know how to teach AT ALL. VERY unclear about how students are being graded and the little feedback provided on assignments was unhelpful and very vague. do not recommend.

One of the best profs. Kind, passionate, knowledgeable, and truly wants you to engage in class. Tight marking, but provided the most clear and valuable feedback I've ever had on my research papers. Pushes you out of comfort zone with public speaking and no references, but provides more than enough notice to prepare. Highly recommend! :)

she's a mediocre prof. very unclear about instructions and criteria for assignments and gives poor feedback. expects way too much for an undergrad course. she's nice tho, just not very helpful. content is repetitive throughout the semester and gets pretty boring.

Dr. Catherine is truly an amazing professor. I would recommend anyone who plans to do research in the future to take her class. While she does grade assignments difficultly, she rewards improvement and really teaches you how to write effectively. Overall one of the most useful classes I have taken in my undergrad.
